mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run distributed machine learning workloads with ease, leveraging Kubernetes' orchestration capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential complexity in managing Ray applications.

steps.title: Add KubeRay Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay easily.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for KubeRay to manage RayCluster, RayJob, and RayService resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of Ray applications.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs the KubeRay operator in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay Installation
steps.description:
After installation, it's important to verify that the KubeRay operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Check that the KubeRay operator pod is running.
> Verify that the CRDs are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the KubeRay operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters, RayJobs, and RayServices managed by KubeRay, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls the KubeRay operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade the KubeRay operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Drains the nodes to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the KubeRay operator.
> Verify that the upgraded operator pod is running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): customresourcedefinitions.apiextensions.k8s.io 'rayclusters.kuberay.io' not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RayCluster CRD was not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RayCluster: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to create the RayCluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has sufficient resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeRay operator pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeRay operator may be misconfigured or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the operator logs using 'kubectl logs <operator-pod-name> -n kuberay' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl following the official installation guide.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features, improved CRD management, and enhanced error handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay operator pod running with a status of 'Running' and the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' should list the operator pod without errors.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
